headline: Ukrainian Drones Strike Moscow Refinery as Israel Deepens Lebanon Incursion
subheadline: Israeli artillery hit Kfar Jouz, Harouf and Al‑Sharqiyah while a senior officer fell; US‑Iran pact reopens Hormuz amid ongoing mine clearance.
lede: Ukrainian unmanned aerial vehicles breached layered air defenses around Moscow, striking the city’s oil refinery and causing minor collateral damage. In southern Lebanon, Israeli artillery and air strikes pounded Kfar Jouz, Harouf and Al‑Sharqiyah, while IDF units remain deployed up to 10 km inside Lebanese territory. Hezbollah claimed a successful attack on an Israeli vehicle, and senior officer Alexander Filin was killed by an explosive device. The United States and Iran signed a memorandum reopening the Strait of Hormuz and initiating mine‑clearance operations.

sections.title: Ukrainian Drones Breach Moscow Defenses, Hit Targets
sections.body: Ukrainian drones reached the Moscow oil refinery, inflicting minor damage and prompting civilian monitoring. Multiple UAVs were also observed over Sumy, Kharkiv, and Chernihiv, heading in various directions. Russian officials reported significant UAV malfunctions on their front lines, reducing operational effectiveness. Russian strategic bombers, including Tu-95MS and Tu-22M3, were detected operating from Olenya, indicating continued long‑range strike capability.

sections.title: Israel Expands Operations Inside Southern Lebanon
sections.body: Israeli artillery struck Kfar Jouz in the Nabatieh district, and airstrikes hit Kfar Jouz, Harouf and Al‑Sharqiyah. Hezbollah fighters claimed to have hit an IDF vehicle during the night. Senior IDF officer Alexander Filin, 29, was killed and seven soldiers wounded by an explosive device during a foot patrol. The IDF announced its operational zone now extends approximately 10 km inside Lebanese territory, focusing on threat removal and civilian protection.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: Ukrainian UAV penetration of Moscow marks a notable escalation in deep‑strike capability, while Russian bomber activity suggests sustained long‑range deterrence. Israel’s extended presence inside Lebanon and the loss of a senior officer heighten the risk of further Hezbollah retaliation, even as the US‑Iran Hormuz agreement eases maritime tensions.
